metadata.name: mgz
metadata.summary: Parse Age of Empires 2 recorded games.

variables.name: mgz.const.VALID_BUILDINGS
documentation: 5th Age, Realms, DE, Various, DE DLC1

modules.name: mgz.body
documentation:
Body.

An mgz body is a stream of Operations

An Operation can be:
 - Action: Player input that materially affects the game
 - Chat: Player chat message
 - Synchronization: Time increment
 - Viewlock: View coordinates of recording player
 - Embedded: A variety of embedded structures

modules.name: mgz.fast
documentation: Fast(er) parsing for situations requiring speed.

functions.name: mgz.fast.parse_action
documentation: Parse player, objects, and coordinates from actions.

modules.name: mgz.body.embedded
documentation:
Embedded Structures.

Various structure can be embedded in the body, without an operation header.

Saved Chapters:

A saved chapter is a header structure embedded in the body.
There is no command identifier, so the command type is actually
the first field of the header - length/offset. Applying the `subheader`
struct at this point will parse the embedded header.


This section is a work in progress.

variables.name: mgz.body.actions.release
documentation:
unload = "unload"/Struct(
    "selected"/Int16ul,
    Padding(1),
    "x"/Float32l, # -1 if none
    "y"/Float32l, # -1 if none
    Padding(4),
    Padding(4), # 0xffffffff
    Array(lambda ctx: ctx.selected, "unit_ids"/Int32ul)
)

variables.name: mgz.body.actions.townbell
documentation:
Patrol

10 X-coordinates followed by 10 Y-coordinates
First of each is popped off for consistency with other actions

variables.name: mgz.body.actions.ai_command
documentation:
DE Queue

In DE queue and multi queue share the same command

variables.name: mgz.body.actions.de_queue
documentation:
DE Attack Move

It's almost the same as Patrol.

10 X-coordinates followed by 10 Y-coordinates
First of each is popped off for consistency with other actions

documentation: UserPatch 1.4+ achievements - appended to recorded game.

functions.name: mgz.summary.players.get_achievements
documentation:
Get achievements for a player.

Must match on name, not index, since order is not always the same.

functions.name: mgz.summary.players.guess_winner
documentation:
Guess if a player won.

Find what team the player was on. If anyone
on their team resigned, assume the player lost.

If there were no resignations, the game ended
by some other condition (wonder, timelimit, etc),
assuming it did complete.

documentation: Compile regular expressions for object searching.

classes.name: mgz.summary.full.FullSummary

methods.name: mgz.summary.full.FullSummary.get_profile_ids

methods.name: mgz.summary.full.FullSummary.get_completed
documentation:
Determine if the game was completed.

If there's a postgame, it will indicate completion.
If there is no postgame, guess based on resignation.

MGZ summary.

Access match summary data.

variables.name: mgz.header.achievements.player_achievements
documentation: Achievements exist for all players but Gaia

functions.name: mgz.util.get_version
documentation: Get version based on version fields.

functions.name: mgz.util.check_flags
documentation: Check byte sequence for only flag bytes.

functions.name: mgz.util.find_postgame
documentation:
Find postgame and grab duration.

We can find postgame location by scanning the last few
thousand bytes of the rec and looking for a pattern as
follows:

[action op]    [action length]    [action type]
01 00 00 00    30 08 00 00        ff

The last occurance of this pattern signals the start of
the postgame structure. Note that the postgame action length
is always constant, unlike other actions.

functions.name: mgz.util.unpack
documentation: Unpack bytes according to format string.

classes.name: mgz.util.Version
documentation:
Version enumeration.

Using consts from https://github.com/goto-bus-stop/recanalyst/blob/master/src/Model/Version.php
for consistency.

classes.name: mgz.util.ZlibCompressed
documentation: Like Compressed, but only does header-less zlib.

classes.name: mgz.util.Find

methods.name: mgz.util.Find._parse

Find bytes, and read past them.

classes.name: mgz.util.RepeatUpTo

methods.name: mgz.util.RepeatUpTo._parse
documentation: Parse until a given byte string is found.
Like RepeatUntil, but doesn't include the last element in the return value.

classes.name: mgz.util.GotoObjectsEnd

methods.name: mgz.util.GotoObjectsEnd._parse
documentation: Parse until the end of objects data.

Necessary since we can't parse objects from a resume game (yet).
